refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR specifies teleoperation latency compatibility with safe remote operation. Refining it to a measurable latency target (p95 ≤ 150 ms) aligns with acceptance criteria and safety criticality.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency under agreed network conditions using telemetry data during teleoperation sessions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the network link between operator and vehicle.
FR2: Ensure real-time processing and forwarding of teleoperation commands and telemetry within the NGHE solution.
FR3: Provide latency monitoring and alerting to detect latency violations.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use optimized network protocols and QoS prioritization for teleoperation data.
DP2: Implement real-time telemetry and command processing pipelines with low-latency buffering.
DP3: Integrate latency monitoring and adaptive control to maintain latency within limits.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use optimized network protocols and QoS prioritization for teleoperation data.

potential_sources: Coupling between network protocol optimization and telemetry processing pipelines.
Dependency on network link quality and vehicle communication hardware.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically addresses the main latency source (network communication) and can be modularized separately from telemetry processing and monitoring.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions meet agreed baseline for latency measurement.
Risk that network QoS mechanisms may not be fully supported by all infrastructure components.
Risk that telemetry processing delays may add to total latency if not optimized.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP2 and DP3 in subsequent refinement levels to address telemetry processing and monitoring explicitly.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation communication, command processing, and latency management.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles bidirectional communication between operator and vehicle for teleoperation data.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Exchanges telemetry data required for latency measurement and control.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope, but teleoperation latency logic is a subset; risk of diffusion if implemented here.
Vehicle module handles physical control but not network latency optimization.
Highest containment score; teleoperation module is the natural owner of latency-related DP.
Safety services monitor alerts but do not directly control latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning DP1 to Teleoperation module centralizes latency optimization logic, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on network infrastructure outside Teleoperation module control.
Latency monitoring may require coordination with Safety Services for alerting.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts with Network Link and Safety Services to manage dependencies and alerting.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (optimized network protocols and QoS) directly addresses the refined FR of minimizing teleoperation latency with clear assumptions and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as DP is contained within Teleoperation module.
Need to monitor dependencies on external network infrastructure.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module has highest containment score and is the logical owner of latency optimization.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified end-to-end using telemetry and network link monitoring within Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of DP to Teleoperation IM with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Plan subsequent refinement to address telemetry processing and latency monitoring (DP2 and DP3).
Clarify interface contracts with Safety Services for latency alerting.

decision.justification: While the main DP and IM assignment are sound, the FR refinement identified sub-FRs that require further decomposition and DP selection to fully cover telemetry processing and latency monitoring aspects.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the network link between operator and vehicle.
FR2: Ensure real-time processing and forwarding of teleoperation commands and telemetry within the NGHE solution.
FR3: Provide latency monitoring and alerting to detect latency violations.

revise.why: To decompose the atomic FR into sub-FRs that enable more detailed DP and IM assignments for telemetry processing and latency monitoring.

assumptions: Teleoperation module can implement required network protocol optimizations.
open_risks: Coordination needed with Safety Services for alerting.
evidence_needed: Latency measurement data under operational conditions.
